While catch_unwind at server layer prevents process crash and parking_lot prevents lock poisoning, panics are expensive and non-idiomatic. Should return Result errors through the FromRequest trait instead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:48:13.868705987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:49:29.056552563Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T22:49:29.056487272Z","close_reason":"Panics are by-design for programmer errors (circular deps, scope violations). Cannot return through generic T::Error type. The parking_lot migration (already applied) prevents cascading failures. catch_unwind at server layer isolates panics to single requests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1bfm","title":"Implement routes table display","description":"## Task: Routes Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 2: Core Output Components (bd-2rso)

### Overview
Create a beautiful routes table display that shows all registered API endpoints in a formatted table when the application starts, similar to FastAPI Python's output but enhanced with rich_rust styling.

### Background & Reasoning
When developers start a web application, seeing all registered routes at a glance provides immediate feedback that routes are configured correctly. Python FastAPI shows routes in a simple list, but we can do better with rich_rust's Table component - showing methods, paths, handler names, and even parameter info in a clean, colored table.

### Technical Implementation

#### RoutesTable Component
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/routes_table.rs

use rich_rust::prelude::*;
use crate::{RichOutput, FastApiTheme};

pub struct RoutesTableConfig {
    pub show_handler_names: bool,
    pub show_parameters: bool,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub max_path_width: usize,
}

impl Default for RoutesTableConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            show_handler_names: true,
            show_parameters: true,
            group_by_tag: false,
            max_path_width: 50,
        }
    }
}

pub struct RouteEntry {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub handler_name: Option<String>,
    pub parameters: Vec<RouteParameter>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
}
```

### HTTP Method Color Scheme
| Method  | Color      |
|---------|------------|
| GET     | Blue       |
| POST    | Green      |
| PUT     | Orange     |
| DELETE  | Red        |
| OPTIONS | Magenta    |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RoutesTableConfig struct with display options
- [ ] RouteEntry and RouteParameter data structures
- [ ] display_routes_table() function with mode awareness
- [ ] HTTP method coloring using theme
- [ ] Parameter formatting with required indicator
- [ ] Integration hook in RouteRegistry
- [ ] Plain text fallback for agent mode
- [ ] Proper column width handling
- [ ] Unit tests for table generation

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme with method colors)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:11:22.165551598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:40:06.263848469Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:40:06.263698788Z","close_reason":"Routes table display implemented in crates/fastapi-output/src/components/routes.rs with RouteEntry, RouteTableConfig, and RouteDisplay types.  {"id":"bd-2rso","title":"Phase 2: Core Output Components - Banner, Logging, Errors, Routes","description":"# Phase 2: Core Output Components

## Purpose

Implement the primary output components that will be used throughout fastapi_rust:
startup banner, request/response logging, error formatting, and route table display.

## Why This Phase

These are the most visible and frequently used output components:
- Banner: First thing users see when server starts
- Logging: Continuous output during operation
- Errors: Critical for debugging and user feedback
- Routes: Essential for understanding API structure

Together, they define the "look and feel" of fastapi_rust.

## Deliverables

### 1. Startup Banner Component
- ASCII art logo for fastapi_rust
- Version, runtime info display
- Server URL and documentation links
- Terminal capability summary

### 2. Request/Response Logger
- HTTP method with color coding
- Path with query highlighting
- Status code with color coding
- Response time display
- Optional verbose mode with headers/body

### 3. Error Formatters
- ValidationErrors with location path display
- HttpError with context
- Panic handler with stack trace
- Internal error wrapping

### 4. Route Table Display
- Method | Path | Handler | Tags columns
- Auto-width calculation
- Method color coding
- Route count summary

## Integration Points

These components integrate with fastapi-core:
- `app.rs`: Startup banner and route table
- `logging.rs`: Request/response logger
- `error.rs`: Error formatters
- `testing.rs`: Test output formatters

## File Organization

```
crates/fastapi-output/src/
├── components/
│   ├── mod.rs          # Component module exports
│   ├── banner.rs       # Startup banner
│   ├── logging.rs      # Request/response logging
│   ├── error.rs        # Error formatters
│   └── routes.rs       # Route table display
```

## Dependencies

- Phase 1 complete (RichOutput facade, themes, mode detection)
- fastapi-core for Request, Response, Error types
- fastapi-router for Route types

## Plain Mode Equivalents

| Rich Output | Plain Mode |
|-------------|------------|
| ASCII art banner | Simple text: "fastapi_rust v0.1.0" |
| Boxed route table | Tab-separated columns |
| Colored method badges | Plain method names |
| Styled error panels | Prefixed error lines |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Banner displays correctly in both modes
2. Request logging is compact and readable
3. Errors show clear location paths
4. Route table auto-sizes to terminal width
5. All components respect OutputMode
6. Performance acceptable for high-throughput logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:20.709114694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:52:10.820497819Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:52:10.820451672Z","close_reason":"Implemented Phase 2 Core Output Components: Banner with ASCII art (rich/plain modes), RequestLogger with method colors and timing, ErrorFormatter for validation/HTTP errors with location paths, RouteDisplay with method-colored tables.  {"id":"bd-2xn1","title":"Implement help and usage display","description":"## Task: Help and Usage Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create beautiful help text and usage information displays for CLI arguments, configuration options, and quick reference guides.

### Background & Reasoning
Good help output is essential for developer experience:
- First impression of the framework
- Reduces friction for new users
- Serves as quick reference
- Conveys quality and attention to detail

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/help_display.rs

pub struct HelpDisplay {
    pub title: String,
    pub version: Option<String>,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub usage: Option<String>,
    pub sections: Vec<HelpSection>,
}

pub struct HelpSection {
    pub name: String,
    pub items: Vec<HelpItem>,
}

pub struct HelpItem {
    pub name: String,
    pub aliases: Vec<String>,      // -h, --help
    pub description: String,
    pub default: Option<String>,
    pub required: bool,
    pub value_hint: Option<String>, // <FILE>, <PORT>
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                              │
│   ███████╗ █████╗ ███████╗████████╗ █████╗ ██████╗ ██╗      │
│   ██╔════╝██╔══██╗██╔════╝╚══██╔══╝██╔══██╗██╔══██╗██║      │
│   █████╗  ███████║███████╗   ██║   ███████║██████╔╝██║      │
│   ██╔══╝  ██╔══██║╚════██║   ██║   ██╔══██║██╔═══╝ ██║      │
│   ██║     ██║  ██║███████║   ██║   ██║  ██║██║     ██║      │
│   ╚═╝     ╚═╝  ╚═╝╚══════╝   ╚═╝   ╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝     ╚═╝      │
│                                                              │
│   Ultra-optimized Rust web framework                 v0.1.0  │
│                                                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Usage: fastapi-app [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Options:
┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ -h, --help         │ Show this help message                 │
│ -V, --version      │ Show version information               │
│ -c, --config <FILE>│ Path to config file [default: app.toml]│
│ -p, --port <PORT>  │ Port to listen on [default: 8000]      │
│ -w, --workers <N>  │ Number of worker threads [default: 4]  │
│     --debug        │ Enable debug output                    │
│ -q, --quiet        │ Suppress non-error output              │
└────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┘

Commands:
┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ serve              │ Start the HTTP server                  │
│ routes             │ Display registered routes              │
│ openapi            │ Generate OpenAPI specification         │
│ check              │ Validate configuration                 │
└────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┘

Examples:
  fastapi-app serve --port 3000
  fastapi-app routes --format json
  fastapi-app openapi > api.yaml
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
fastapi - Ultra-optimized Rust web framework v0.1.0

Usage: fastapi-app [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Options:
  -h, --help           Show this help message
  -V, --version        Show version information
  -c, --config <FILE>  Path to config file [default: app.toml]
  -p, --port <PORT>    Port to listen on [default: 8000]
  -w, --workers <N>    Number of worker threads [default: 4]
      --debug          Enable debug output
  -q, --quiet          Suppress non-error output

Commands:
  serve    Start the HTTP server
  routes   Display registered routes
  openapi  Generate OpenAPI specification
  check    Validate configuration
```

### Implementation
```rust
impl HelpDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        
        // Banner (optional ASCII art)
        if let Some(banner) = self.render_banner(theme) {
            output.print(&banner);
        }
        
        // Usage line
        if let Some(usage) = &self.usage {
            output.print(&format!("Usage: {}", theme.style_text(usage, "bold")));
            output.print("");
        }
        
        // Sections
        for section in &self.sections {
            output.print(&theme.style_text(&format!("{}:", section.name), "bold"));
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(20))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(50));
            
            for item in &section.items {
                let name_str = self.format_item_name(item, theme);
                let desc_str = self.format_item_description(item, theme);
                
                table.add_row_cells([name_str, desc_str]);
            }
            
            output.renderable(&table);
            output.print("");
        }
    }
    
    fn format_item_name(&self, item: &HelpItem, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> String {
        let mut parts = Vec::new();
        
        // Short alias first
        if let Some(short) = item.aliases.first() {
            parts.push(theme.style_text(short, "cyan"));
        }
        
        // Long name
        parts.push(theme.style_text(&format!("--{}", item.name), "cyan"));
        
        // Value hint
        if let Some(hint) = &item.value_hint {
            parts.push(theme.style_text(&format!(" <{}>", hint), "dim"));
        }
        
        parts.join(", ")
    }
    
    fn format_item_description(&self, item: &HelpItem, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> String {
        let mut desc = item.description.clone();
        
        if let Some(default) = &item.default {
            desc = format!("{} [default: {}]", desc, default);
        }
        
        if item.required {
            desc = format!("{} {}", desc, theme.style_text("(required)", "yellow"));
        }
        
        desc
    }
    
    fn render_banner(&self, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> Option<String> {
        // Simplified ASCII art for the banner
        Some(theme.style_text(&format!(
            "\n  {} v{}\n  {}\n",
            self.title,
            self.version.as_deref().unwrap_or("0.0.0"),
            self.description.as_deref().unwrap_or("")
        ), "cyan bold"))
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```rust
// In fastapi/src/cli.rs

pub fn print_help() {
    HelpDisplay {
        title: "fastapi".to_string(),
        version: Some(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string()),
        description: Some("Ultra-optimized Rust web framework".to_string()),
        usage: Some("fastapi-app [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]".to_string()),
        sections: vec![
            HelpSection {
                name: "Options".to_string(),
                items: vec![
                    HelpItem {
                        name: "help".to_string(),
                        aliases: vec!["-h".to_string()],
                        description: "Show this help message".to_string(),
                        ..Default::default()
                    },
                    // ... more options
                ],
            },
            HelpSection {
                name: "Commands".to_string(),
                items: vec![
                    HelpItem {
                        name: "serve".to_string(),
                        description: "Start the HTTP server".to_string(),
                        ..Default::default()
                    },
                    // ... more commands
                ],
            },
        ],
    }.display();
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HelpDisplay, HelpSection, HelpItem structs
- [ ] Option name formatting with aliases
- [ ] Value hints display
- [ ] Default value display
- [ ] Required indicator
- [ ] Section grouping
- [ ] Table layout for options
- [ ] Banner/logo display (optional)
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Examples section support
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- bd-urty (Phase 5 feature)

### Considerations
- Banner should be optional and skippable
- Very long descriptions should wrap properly
- Consider subcommand help recursion
- Support for examples section","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:19:43.270045296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:11:28.085847501Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T02:11:28.085768033Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete in help_display.rs - HelpDisplay, ArgInfo, CommandInfo, HelpInfo structs with builder pattern, option formatting with short/long names, value hints, default values, required indicators, section grouping, plain text fallback, and 5 unit tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xn1","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xn1","depends_on_id":"bd-36uw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xn1","depends_on_id":"bd-urty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yfj","title":"Implement integration tests","description":"## Task: Integration Tests (REVISED)

### Overview
Create integration tests that verify components work correctly together in realistic scenarios using the ACTUAL implemented API.

### Background
Integration tests ensure the complete system works:
- Components integrate correctly
- Data flows properly between layers
- Real-world scenarios are covered

### Test Scenarios Using Actual API

#### 1. Complete Startup Flow
```rust
use fastapi_output::prelude::*;
use fastapi_output::testing::*;
use serial_test::serial;

#[test]
#[serial]
fn test_complete_startup_sequence() {
    with_clean_env(|| {
        let captured = capture(OutputMode::Plain, || {
            let output = RichOutput::auto();

            // Banner
            let banner = Banner::new(output.mode());
            let info = ServerInfo::new("1.0.0", "localhost", 8000)
                .docs_path("/docs")
                .redoc_path("/redoc");
            println!("{}", banner.render(&info));

            // Status messages
            output.success("Server initialized");
            output.info("Loading routes...");
            output.success("5 routes loaded");
        });

        assert_no_ansi(&captured);
        assert_contains(&captured, "FastAPI Rust");
        assert_contains(&captured, "Server initialized");
        assert_contains_in_order(&captured, &["initialized", "Loading", "loaded"]);
    });
}
```

#### 2. Request/Response Logging Flow
```rust
#[test]
#[serial]
fn test_request_response_logging_flow() {
    with_clean_env(|| {
        let logger = RequestLogger::new(OutputMode::Plain);

        let entries = vec![
            LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Get, "/api/users", 200)
                .timing(ResponseTiming::new(Duration::from_millis(15))),
            LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Post, "/api/users", 201)
                .timing(ResponseTiming::new(Duration::from_millis(45))),
            LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Get, "/api/users/42", 200)
                .timing(ResponseTiming::new(Duration::from_millis(12))),
        ];

        let mut output = String::new();
        for entry in &entries {
            output.push_str(&logger.format(entry));
            output.push('\n');
        }

        assert_no_ansi(&output);
        assert_contains(&output, "GET");
        assert_contains(&output, "POST");
        assert_contains(&output, "200");
        assert_contains(&output, "201");
    });
}
```

#### 3. Error Handling Flow
```rust
#[test]
#[serial]
fn test_error_handling_flow() {
    with_clean_env(|| {
        let formatter = ErrorFormatter::new(OutputMode::Plain);

        // Validation errors
        let val_errors = vec![
            ValidationErrorDetail::new(
                vec![LocItem::field("body"), LocItem::field("email")],
                "invalid email format",
                "value_error.email",
            ),
        ];
        let val_result = formatter.format_validation_errors(&val_errors);

        assert_no_ansi(&val_result.plain);
        assert_contains(&val_result.plain, "Validation Error");
        assert_contains(&val_result.plain, "body.email");

        // HTTP errors
        let http_err = HttpErrorInfo::new(404, "User not found")
            .path("/api/users/999")
            .method("GET");
        let http_result = formatter.format_http_error(&http_err);

        assert_no_ansi(&http_result.plain);
        assert_contains(&http_result.plain, "404");
        assert_contains(&http_result.plain, "Not Found");
    });
}
```

#### 4. Agent Mode Detection Integration
```rust
#[test]
#[serial]
fn test_agent_mode_affects_all_components() {
    with_clean_env(|| {
        set_env("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");

        let output = RichOutput::auto();
        assert!(output.is_agent_mode());

        // Banner should be plain
        let banner = Banner::new(output.mode());
        let banner_out = banner.render(&ServerInfo::default());
        assert_no_ansi(&banner_out);

        // Logger should be plain
        let logger = RequestLogger::new(output.mode());
        let log_out = logger.format(&LogEntry::new(HttpMethod::Get, "/", 200));
        assert_no_ansi(&log_out);

        // Errors should be plain
        let formatter = ErrorFormatter::new(output.mode());
        let err_out = formatter.format_simple("Test error");
        assert_no_ansi(&err_out.plain);
    });
}
```

#### 5. Theme Consistency Integration
```rust
#[test]
fn test_theme_applies_consistently() {
    let theme = FastApiTheme::default();
    let output = RichOutput::builder()
        .mode(OutputMode::Rich)
        .theme(theme.clone())
        .build();

    // Theme should be accessible from output
    assert!(!output.theme().success.to_hex().is_empty());

    // Banner with theme
    let banner = Banner::new(OutputMode::Rich).theme(theme);
    let banner_out = banner.render(&ServerInfo::default());

    // Rich mode should have ANSI codes
    assert!(banner_out.contains("\x1b["));
}
```

### Test Logging
All integration tests should include detailed logging:
```rust
eprintln!("[INTEG] Testing: {}", test_name);
eprintln!("[INTEG] Setup: {}", setup_description);
eprintln!("[INTEG] Output:\n{}", captured_output);
eprintln!("[INTEG] PASS: {}", test_name);
```

### Run Command
```bash
cargo test --package fastapi-output integration -- --nocapture 2>&1 | grep INTEG
```
